candidate_problems.text: Distinguish how much of the antibiotic asymmetry is due to evolutionary population size and generation time versus human institutional suppression of exploratory variation; the conjecture could be wrong about which is primary.
Identify drug-design strategies that are genuinely hard for bacterial systems to vary against, rather than merely slower to evade under current conditions.
Test whether decentralized, high-variation discovery institutions would actually outperform current pipelines once safety, manufacturing, and clinical constraints are included.
